research.title: IMP_02 Code Audit — Gap Analysis
research.topic: What is implemented vs what IMP_02.md requires
research.summary: Audited all backend modules, frontend pages, RBAC, priorities against IMP_02.md spec. Core functionality 85% complete. Key gaps: priority reorder UI, read-only lock during auto-buy, route-level 403 guards, granular RBAC permissions, My Customers hardcoded countries. 6 open items resolved by code (OI-P01/P03/P05/P06), 3 deferred (OI-07/OI-13/OI-P04), 2 need decisions (OI-01/OI-06).
key_findings: Purchase Priorities: backend Step 3 complete, API has GET/POST/DELETE but no PUT or reorder endpoint, frontend lacks up/down controls and auto-buy lock
RBAC: 5 roles seeded with correct permissions, country scoping via scope_country works, but Back Office is unscoped (should be scoped per OI-06 recommendation)
Frontend: all pages exist and functional, but only Admin has 403 route guard, My Customers has hardcoded country list, menu not filtered by role
Audit trail: 19 action types, log_audit used across all modules, unauthorized access attempts NOT logged
Notifications: 4 types defined, SMTP not configured (fallback to logging)
OI-01 CRITICAL: ITRP Manager has maintenance:write but docs conflict — recommend keeping write + splitting into granular permissions
Seed data: 12 countries, 66 OpCos, 5 demo users, 3 test priorities

research.title: IMP_02.md Full Ingestion
research.topic: Extract all implementable facts from IMP_02.md + code audit
research.summary: Extracted 11 requirements (K-003..K-013) and 6 domain rules (K-014..K-019) from IMP_02.md. Covered: Purchase Priorities (6 BR, UI, API, pipeline), RBAC (5 roles, per-page matrix, country scoping), Audit Trail (access denied logging), Concurrency (auto-buy lock). Code audit confirmed: Step 3 complete, RBAC infrastructure complete, gaps in frontend guards and priority UI.
key_findings: 11 requirements extracted covering priorities, RBAC, country scoping, audit, concurrency
6 domain rules covering priority business logic and RBAC model
Code has 85% coverage — main gaps: priority reorder UI, route guards, menu visibility, Back Office scoping
6 decisions resolved (D-016..D-021), 3 still open from earlier phases (D-004, D-005, D-006)

research.title: Ingestion: SPEC-auto-buy-schedule-maintenance.md
research.topic: Extract structured facts from schedule maintenance specification
research.summary: Extracted 21 requirements (REQ-SCH01-21), 7 business rules (BR-SCH), 3 guidelines (G-010,G-012,G-013), 1 API reference. Trust: HIGH. Full feature spec with API contracts, data model, AC.
key_findings: 7 API endpoints fully specified with request/response schemas
Bulk PUT + per-country PATCH both specified
Add Country modal with ISO validation (GFI only)
4 frontend validation rules (FV-01 to FV-04)
10 open items (OI-01 resolved by D-022)
file_day_basis field is obsolete per ARCH-007

research.title: Ingestion: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md
research.topic: Extract structured facts from auto-buy pipeline analysis
research.summary: Extracted 10 requirements (REQ-AB01-10), 3 domain rules (eligibility, rollback, auto-disable), 1 notification spec, 1 schedule config. Trust: HIGH. Covers full Steps 0-9 pipeline with business rules.
key_findings: 10-step pipeline (Steps 0-9) fully documented
8 eligibility rules with override matrix
Credit limit breach pauses job (not auto-exclude)
All-or-nothing rollback on any failure
6 notification event types
Warsaw API = stub (no spec available)

research.title: Ingestion: SCHEDULER-CONFIGURATION-analysis.md
research.topic: Extract structured facts from scheduler configuration analysis
research.summary: Extracted 2 architecture facts (Cloud Scheduler + Trigger Service), 1 technical context (data model). Trust: HIGH. Covers infra-level scheduling, DB-driven config, file-completion gate.
key_findings: 4 day-based Cloud Scheduler jobs (not per-country)
Trigger Service resolves countries at runtime from DB
File-completion gate mechanism undefined (OI-05)
Data fetch is event-driven (not cron)
Master data sync is fixed cron (no FE config)

research.title: Ingestion: RBAC-roles-analysis.md
research.topic: Extract structured facts from RBAC roles analysis
research.summary: Extracted 1 guideline (G-012 server-side RBAC), confirmed 5 roles. Trust: HIGH. Permission matrix 5x5. 3 open items (Mid Office confirmation, permission granularity, admin role).
key_findings: 5 roles confirmed: ITRP Manager, Back Office, Mid Office (TBC), OpCo, Global Finance IT
ITRP Manager Maintenance access: Read in matrix, Write in interactions (resolved by D-022: Write)
Mid Office role existence still TBC
Global Finance IT = platform oversight, full maintenance+audit
